title: frontend: Add @typescript/native-preview and use it for type checking
author: sniok

findings.description: @typescript/native-preview is pinned to a nightly dev snapshot (7.0.0-dev.20260519.1). Nightly packages can be unpublished or have breaking type-checker differences from stable TypeScript. Consider keeping typescript for CI and using tsgo as an opt-in alias.
Replacing 'tsc': 'tsc' with 'tsc': 'tsgo' means all CI and contributor workflows now use the experimental compiler. Any tsgo-specific quirk becomes a silent CI breakage.

Changing moduleResolution from 'node' to 'bundler' creates a discrepancy between IDE TypeScript integration (which uses the installed typescript package) and CLI type-checking. May affect downstream plugin authors using the same tsconfig.
The semver range ^7.0.0-dev.20260519.1 will not auto-upgrade to newer dev snapshots due to npm's conservative pre-release caret handling. Document explicitly.
5x type-check speedup is compelling for developer DX. Exclusion from plugin dependency sync is correct.
